commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The PR needs author-visible code and proof updates; ClawSweeper repair should not take over while the external real-behavior proof gate remains insufficient.; Needs attention: The diff improves an untrusted network read cap, but it currently accepts truncated HTML as complete provider data.; Review finding: [P2] Handle truncated DuckDuckGo bodies before parsing — extensions/duckduckgo/src/ddg-client.ts:205-207 (sha=ba13e3c4c62ec8055e83e073bd802412230c5182)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the DuckDuckGo client success path from unbounded `response.text()` to `readResponseText(response, { maxBytes: 2 * 1024 * 1024 })`.

PR surface: Source +2. Total +2 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main uses unbounded `response.text()` on the DuckDuckGo success path, and the bounded helper returns `truncated` instead of throwing. I did not run live DuckDuckGo or the PR proof in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs author-visible code and proof updates; ClawSweeper repair should not take over while the external real-behavior proof gate remains insufficient.

target.title: fix(xai): rediscover retired OAuth token endpoint
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Issue https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84018 reports xAI OAuth refresh hitting `https://auth.x.ai/oauth/token` and receiving a Cloudflare CAPTCHA/HTML response. I did not find evidence that `/oauth/token` is a supported xAI endpoint or that OpenClaw source historically hardcoded it for xAI, but OpenClaw refresh does trust a persisted credential `tokenEndpoint` before rediscovering current metadata.

Current xAI discovery returns `https://auth.x.ai/oauth2/token`. If a saved credential contains the bad/stale `/oauth/token` endpoint, refresh keeps posting there and receives Cloudflare HTML instead of OAuth JSON.

## Why This Change Was Made

`extensions/xai/xai-oauth.ts` already stores the discovered endpoint for new OAuth and device-code credentials. This change only special-cases the known unsupported xAI `/oauth/token` endpoint during refresh: if a credential has `/oauth/token`, OpenClaw rediscovers the current token endpoint before posting, and the successful refresh writes the canonical `/oauth2/token` endpoint back into the credential.

Minimal live repro with fake tokens, current endpoint:

```bash
curl -sS -o /dev/null -X POST "https://auth.x.ai/oauth2/token" -H "Accept: application/json" -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" --data "grant_type=refresh_token&client_id=b1a00492-073a-47ea-816f-4c329264a828&refresh_token=not-a-real-refresh-token" -w "http_code=%{http_code} content_type=%{content_type}\n"
```

Minimal live repro with fake tokens, reported unsupported endpoint:

```bash
curl -sS -o /dev/null -X POST "https://auth.x.ai/oauth/token" -H "Accept: application/json" -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" --data "grant_type=refresh_token&client_id=b1a00492-073a-47ea-816f-4c329264a828&refresh_token=not-a-real-refresh-token" -w "http_code=%{http_code} content_type=%{content_type}\n"
```

Observed live result while preparing this PR:

```text
https://auth.x.ai/oauth2/token -> 400 application/json, OAuth error invalid_grant
https://auth.x.ai/oauth/token  -> 403 text/html; charset=UTF-8, server cloudflare
```

## User Impact

Users with stale or bad persisted xAI OAuth credentials no longer get stuck on the unsupported Cloudflare-served endpoint. Their next refresh attempts the current endpoint from xAI discovery and normalizes the stored endpoint after a successful refresh.

## Evidence

- `pnpm test extensions/xai/xai-oauth.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check`
- Full `pnpm test` was attempted and failed in unrelated broad-suite areas after this PR's targeted test, build, and check passed.
- Live dummy POST probes above confirmed `/oauth2/token` returns OAuth JSON while `/oauth/token` returns Cloudflare HTML.
target.author: jaaneek
